steps.title: Find calcium from milk
steps.content:
The recipe uses $4$ cups of milk, and each cup has $286$ milligrams of calcium.
$$4(286)=1{,}144$$
So the milk contributes $1{,}144$ milligrams of calcium.

steps.title: Find calcium from bananas
steps.content:
The whole recipe has $1{,}158$ milligrams of calcium. Subtract the milk calcium.
$$1{,}158-1{,}144=14$$
The two bananas together contribute $14$ milligrams.

steps.title: Divide by two bananas
steps.content:
Each banana has half of that amount.
$$\frac{14}{2}=7$$
The answer is D.
